what_it_does: Implements stdlib-only HTTP transports for OpenAI-compatible chat completions, native ollama chat, model listing, tool-capability probing, and embeddings. Each public factory returns a closure that serializes a JSON request via urllib, parses the response, and returns a content string, message dict, or list of embedding vectors.
observations: Uses only urllib.request and json from the standard library.
Each transport is built by a factory closure bound to base_url, api_key, and timeout.
Hard constraint against Gemini is enforced via forbid_gemini on every entry point.
Sampling options are forwarded through an ordered allowlist _SAMPLING_KEYS, not a denylist.
Structured output is selected by json_schema first, then schema/json, producing json_schema or json_object response_format.
An optional meta dict in opts receives finish_reason and usage without changing the transport return type.
Native ollama transport targets /api/chat, strips /v1 from the base URL, maps max_tokens to options.num_predict, and carries a think flag.
Tool capability detection is endpoint-aware and raises when it cannot determine capability.
Embeddings transport speaks /v1/embeddings and returns the bare vector list.
standouts: The ordered sampling allowlist is explicitly designed to keep existing callers byte-identical.
A separate native ollama path exists solely because OpenAI-compatible endpoints silently ignore think.
Tool capability is probed or read from endpoint metadata rather than assumed.
concerns: Dense, ticket-heavy comments may drift from the code over time.
ollama_native_transport structured output is documented as inconsistent on cloud reasoning models.
model_supports_tools for vllm consumes a real chat completion probe.
There is no explicit handling of HTTP status codes beyond urllib.urlopen raising on non-2xx.
notes: A small stdlib-only HTTP bridge that centralizes OpenAI-shaped request building, ollama-native chat, embeddings, and honest tool-capability probing for fabric.
questions: What are the actual values of DEFAULT_TIMEOUT, DEFAULT_BASE_URL, and DEFAULT_EMBED_URL in fabric.config?
Does forbid_gemini raise an exception or return a filtered result?
Which callers consume the dict returned by openai_tools_transport?
How are retries and error recovery layered around these transports?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—an OpenAI-compatible transport with ollama-native and embeddings siblings—matches the implemented functions.
intention_signals: It is trying to provide a single-source, reusable HTTP transport layer where new sampling options and structured-output modes can be added without touching every caller, while refusing to guess model capabilities.
novelty: The explicit ordered sampling allowlist designed to preserve byte-identical requests for existing callers, plus a dedicated native ollama path solely to carry the think flag that OpenAI-shaped endpoints drop.
contribution: A set of focused transport closures and capability probes that centralize how fabric serializes requests and parses OpenAI/ollama response shapes, keeping callers isolated from endpoint-specific HTTP details.
summary_for_embedding: A stdlib-only OpenAI-compatible HTTP transport module exposing chat, tool-chat, native ollama, embeddings, model listing, and tool-capability probing functions for the fabric project.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a reusable, fail-loud HTTP bridge between fabric callers and OpenAI-compatible or ollama-native endpoints with controlled option forwarding and structured-output support.

symbols.does: Lists registered models at an OpenAI-compatible /v1/models endpoint, excluding Gemini.
symbols.description: Sends an authorized GET request to base_url/models, parses the data array of model objects, and returns the id values filtered to remove any containing 'gemini'.

symbols.does: Forwards present allowlisted sampling keys from opts into the request body.
symbols.description: Iterates the ordered _SAMPLING_KEYS tuple and copies each key that exists in opts into body, preserving insertion order so that requests remain byte-identical for callers not passing new keys.

symbols.does: Sets body['response_format'] based on structured-output options.
symbols.description: Prefers opts['json_schema'] to produce a typed json_schema response_format, falls back to json_object when opts contains schema or json, and otherwise leaves the body unchanged.

symbols.does: Writes finish_reason and usage into an optional meta out-param dict.
symbols.description: If opts contains a meta dict, populates it from the OpenAI response envelope choices and usage fields without altering the transport's return value.

symbols.does: Factory returning an OpenAI /v1/chat/completions transport that returns a content string.
symbols.description: Builds a closure that forbids Gemini, constructs the request body with response_format and sampling options, posts JSON to /chat/completions, fills meta, and returns choices[0].message.content.

symbols.does: Inner closure performing a chat completion and returning the content string.
symbols.description: Serializes the model and messages into an OpenAI request, sends it, parses the response, optionally fills meta, and returns the bare assistant content.

symbols.does: Factory returning an OpenAI /v1/chat/completions transport that returns the full message dict.
symbols.description: Builds a closure similar to openai_transport but adds tools and tool_choice to the body when tools are present, and returns the whole message dict including any tool_calls.

symbols.does: Inner closure performing a tool-aware chat completion and returning the message dict.
symbols.description: Constructs and sends the OpenAI request with optional tool fields, then returns choices[0].message so callers can see content and tool_calls together.

symbols.does: Factory returning an ollama /api/chat transport with think control.
symbols.description: Builds a closure that strips /v1 from the base URL, maps max_tokens to options.num_predict, carries a think flag, sets best-effort format, and returns message.content.

symbols.does: Inner closure performing a native ollama chat and returning the content string.
symbols.description: Sends a request to /api/chat with think and options, parses the native response shape, optionally fills meta with done_reason and token counts, and returns message.content.

symbols.does: Endpoint-aware probe that returns whether a model supports native tools.
symbols.description: Queries ollama /api/show capabilities, LiteLLM /model_info, or forces a vLLM tool call; raises ValueError when capability cannot be determined.

symbols.does: Factory returning an OpenAI /v1/embeddings transport that returns vectors.
symbols.description: Builds a closure that posts a batch of input strings to /embeddings and returns the list of embedding vectors from the data array.

symbols.does: Inner closure performing an embeddings request and returning a list of vectors.
symbols.description: Serializes the model and input list, sends to /v1/embeddings, parses the response, and returns each entry's embedding array in order.
